schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

address.streetAddress: G-131, Ground Floor, Phase 3, Spencer Plaza Mall, Anna Salai
Workafella, AK Estate, SV Road, off Veer Savarkar Flyover, Goregaon West

schemas.name: GST Annual Return GSTR-9 Filing
schemas.description: GSTR-9 annual return filing by CA firm. Reconciliation of monthly returns with annual financials. GSTR-9C certification for turnover above \u20b95 crore. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: GST Annual Return (GSTR-9)
schemas.description: GSTR-9 annual return filing by CA firm. Reconciliation of monthly returns with annual financials. GSTR-9C certification for turnover above \u20b95 crore. Virtual Auditor.

schemas.name: GST Annual Return (GSTR-9)
schemas.description: GSTR-9 annual return filing by CA firm. Reconciliation of monthly returns with annual financials. GSTR-9C certification for turnover above \u20b95 crore. Virtual Auditor.

step.text: Reconcile GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B for the year
step.name: Step 1

step.text: File on GST portal by December 31
step.name: Step 5

acceptedAnswer.text: All regular GST-registered taxpayers. Exempt: composition dealers (file GSTR-9A), casual taxable persons, non-resident taxable persons, and ISD. Due date: December 31 of the following financial year.

For FY 2023-24 onwards: self-certification required for taxpayers with aggregate turnover exceeding \u20b95 crore. No CA certification needed (changed from earlier requirement). But CA review is still recommended to ensure accuracy.

mainEntity.name: What if monthly returns have errors?

acceptedAnswer.text: GSTR-9 provides an opportunity to report corrections (additional liability, ITC adjustments). However, it cannot be revised once filed. We reconcile all 12 months of GSTR-1/3B against books before filing GSTR-9.

Yes, but only for taxpayers with aggregate turnover exceeding ₹5 crore. It is now a self-certified reconciliation statement (not CA-certified since FY 2020-21). Must reconcile GSTR-9 with audited financials.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for late GSTR-9?

acceptedAnswer.text: Late fee: ₹200/day (₹100 CGST + ₹100 SGST), maximum 0.50% of turnover (0.25% CGST + 0.25% SGST). For ₹1 crore turnover: maximum late fee ₹50,000. File before GSTR-9 due date (December 31).

mainEntity.name: Can errors in GSTR-1/3B be corrected in GSTR-9?

acceptedAnswer.text: GSTR-9 allows reporting of amendments and corrections to monthly returns. However, it is not a mechanism to pay additional tax. Any additional liability discovered must be paid through DRC-03 before filing GSTR-9.

mainEntity.name: What reconciliation is needed for GSTR-9?

acceptedAnswer.text: Reconcile: GSTR-1 (outward supply) vs books, GSTR-3B (tax payment) vs books, ITC claimed vs ITC eligible (GSTR-2B), expenses in P&L vs GST returns, and turnover per ITR vs turnover per GST.
